text:
It was another great day for Team GB on Tuesday with two more gold medals at the2024 Paris Olympics. Nathan Hales took glory bysetting a new Games record in the men's trap shooting final, while in the pool Matt Richards, James Guy, Tom Dean and Duncan Scott defended their 4x200m freestyle relay title.

And the dream continues forAndy Murrayand Dan Evans,who saved two match points in an epic 6-3 6-7 (8-10) 11-9 winover Belgium's Sander Gille and Joran Vliegen in the second round.

Team GB will be looking to add more medals today in the morning triathlon events. The competition will be allowed to take place after tests showed the River Seine to be clean enough athletes to enter.

Express Sport brings you the latest Paris Olympics news in our LIVE blog, below…

Norwegian swimmer Henrik Christiansen has broken his silence after being outed as the Olympic village's 'muffin man'.

The three-time Olympian had beenaccused of eating all the chocolate muffins inside the accommodation, forcing him to post a video explaining his actions.

"Guys, I think I have a problem," one of his TikToks began, capturing him in bed next to an open drawer of the bakery product.

So those hoping to catch a glimpse of the chocolate muffin inside the Olympic village, you better be quicker than one of the world's top swimmers.

Leon Marchand has won two gold medals in the space of two hours.

The home favourite took gold in the 200m butterfly earlier this evening, defeating Hungary's world and Olympic record holder Kristof Malik.

Then he rocked up once again for the 200m breaststroke, blitzing the competition tobecome the first competitor to win two swimming golds in one night since 1976.

Not even swimming's greatest-ever, Michael Phelps, has matched Marchand's achievement tonight, sending his compatriots into a frenzy.

Olympic venue Yves-du-Manoir Stadium had to be evacuated due to adverse weather conditions in Paris, with hockey fans told to leave the arena.

But once all supporters had exited, play between Spain vs South Africa and China vs the Netherlands resumed,players seemingly did not need to heed warnings from officials.

The FIH issued a statement which read: "Following a warning from Meteo France of imminent storms, a decision has been taken by the venue and the local authorities to evacuate the stadium in the interests of public safety. The matches today are played as per the schedule."

Marta, who is likely playing in her final match for Brazil, left the pitch in tears after she was sent off for a flying kick on Olga Carmona.

On her 200th and perhaps last cap, Marta was inconsolable as she left the pitch, after announcing that she will retire from international football this year.

After going down to 10 players in the sixth minute through Marta's dismissal, Brazil lost 2-0 to Spain and she will be suspended for their quarter-final match.

If Brazil lose, then Marta's last game for her country after a legendary career will have ended in just a few minutes through an ugly red card.

Rafael Nadal's time at the Olympics is over, as he along with fellow Spanish starCarlos Alcarazlost 6-2 6-4 to No. 4 seeds Austin Krajicek and Rajeev Ram.

Nadal's future is now uncertain, having been tipped to retire following the Olympics, though Alcaraz will still be competing in the singles competition.

Despite their immense success in singles Grand Slams, Nadal and Alcaraz wereunable to match the quality of their American double-specialist opponentson Court Philippe-Chatrier.



Japan's Shinnosuke Oka celebrated as he just about pipped China's Boheng Zang to the men's gymnastics all-around gold.

He won by just 0.233 as Oka's compatriot Xiao Ruoteng took bronze.

Team GB's Joe Fraser was fifth and Jake Jarman seventh in an impressive showing of British gymnastics.

The women's windsurfing marathon had to be abandoned in Marseille today, in the event's first year as an Olympic competition, due to lack of wind.

The competitors got underway but withIsrael's Sharon Kantor leading the way, the race was abandoned and won't be re-run today.

The men's event was similarly postponed, but took place later in the day. Team GB's Emma Wilson had been leading the standings going into today's race.

Unfortunately, the marathon had to be abandoned due to a lack of wind \ud83d\udd1a Fate can shift in an instant. It's all part of the game \ud83c\udfb2We will keep you updated on what happens next.#Paris2024Sailing#Paris2024#Sailing#Olympicspic.twitter.com/f1L4Zr7ej5

Olympic chiefs may already have confirmed thatAndy Murraywon't get any fanfare when he ends his career in Paris.

No athletes at the Games are allowed any celebration of their careers, including on-court farewell speeches often found in tennis.

That was compounded today when former world No. 1 andWimbledonchampion Angelique Kerber's career came to an end against Qinwen Zheng.

She wasnot afforded the opportunity to say goodbye to the crowd, instead offered a simple wave before leaving the court for the last time.

And Murray's exit from tennis could follow in the same manner.



Felix Dolci was involved in scary scenes when his handguard split in two when he was preparing to dismount,causing him to fall from the bars onto the mat.

The arena let out a huge gasp as the Canadian struggled to get to his feet, holding his bloodied hand.

Dolci got to his feet and discussed the prospect of returning to the competition, doing so a few moments later with a sore hand - but fell again in another sore landing.

He then took the bars again to finish, scoring 11.733 to huge applause as those in Paris recognised the incredible mental toughness needed to carry on.

Australian Jesse Moore then suffered a second fall of the competition, landing awkwardly on his neck, but was able to return to action.

Cindy Ngamba, a Bolton-based boxer representing the Refugee Olympic Team, has defeated number three seed Tammara Thibeault to advance to the quarter-finals.

The women's 75kg star left Cameroon for Britain at the age of 11 and is a three-time English champion, but is not able to compete for Team GB after being denied citizenship.

There is hope that she will be able to represent Team GB in four years' time, as she trains and travels with Team GB players and coaches.

Ngamba was a round down but clawed back to defeat the 2022 World Championships gold medallist on points, before celebrating with British coaches.

Adriana Ruano Oliva has become the first-ever Guatemalan to win an Olympic gold medalist, also securing a new Games record in the women's trap shooting final.

The 42-year-old made 45 hits out of 50 shots, while Italy's Silvana Maria Stanco took silver, with Australia's Penny Smith claiming bronze.



So close! A score of 93.91 moves Great Britain's Kieran Reilly up to second spot at the end of the BMX freestyle final.

Argetina's Jose Torres Gil takes gold and it's bronze for France's Anthony Jeanjean.

There have been two big crashes in the BMX freestyle final.

Team GB's Kieran Reilly is in second place after his first run though.

Gold medal contender Shericka Jackson has withdrawn from the 100 meters at the2024 Paris Olympics.

Jamaican track and field team manager Ludlow Watts told reuters: "All the information that we can provide is that she is not going to participate in the 100 meters.I don't know the reasons. I'm just dealing with what is the outcome."

READ MORE HERE.

What a start to the Olympics for the women's Team GB hockey side too.

While all that hockey and rowing drama was going on, they came from behind to beat South Africa 2-1 this morning.



An emotional Lola Anderson cried as she revealed how her late father kept her Olympics 2024 dream alive.

After winning in the women’s quadruple sculls with Lauren Henry, Hannah Scott and Georgie Brayshaw, she told theBBC: “It feels like it’s been ages working towards this. We were sticking to what comes next but it comes to the end of the cycle and it doesn’t get bigger. It’s been overwhelming to experience this but just incredible.”

Asked about writing on a piece of paper she wanted this moment to come, she replied: “13 years ago. I’d forgotten about it but a couple of years ago my dad reminded me and I know that he would be so, so proud. I’m thinking a lot about him right now.”

READ MORE HERE.

Lauren Henry, Hannah Scott, Lola Anderson and Georgie Brayshaw win gold for Team GB in the women's quad sculls final.

It required a photo finish as the Dutch led for the majority of the race but Team GB fought back.

What an incredible comeback for Alex Yee as he wins gold for Team GB.

New Zealand's Hayden Wilde opened up a gap of 15 seconds up front but Yee clearly managed his energy levels well and surged ahead in the final 1km.

READ MORE HERE.

Alex Yee is sitting second in the men's triathlon during the men's race.

South Africa's Jamie Riddle leads for now. Another Team GB athlete, Sam Dickinson, is also in contention.

The lowdown on our best medal hopes for the rest of Wednesday:

9.45am:Men's Individual Triathlon (Alex Yee, Samuel Dickinson);10am:Women's Synchronised 10m Platform Final (Lois Toulson and Andrea Spendolini-Sirieix);11.26am:Men's Quad Sculls Finals (Team GB);11.38am:Women's Quad Sculls Finals (Team GB);1.44pm:Cycling BMX Freestyle Men's Final (Kieran Rielly);3pm:Judo, Women's 70kg and Men's 90kg finals (Katie-Jemima and Yeats Brown);4.30pm:Men's Gymnastics All-Around Final (Team GB).

Norwegian athlete Lotte Miller required medical attention after being sent flying off her bike for the second time during the women'sOlympictriathlon

Miller got up and continued after her first crash but was unable to carry on after being sent tumbling to the ground a second time. She looked to be in pain after hitting the deck as police officers rushed to her aid.

READ MORE HERE.

A first Olympic medal for Beth Potter!

In a brutal race, she held on to third as Cassandre Beaugrand took gold in her home city in 1:54:55.

Switzerland's Julie Derron claimed silver.

READ MORE HERE.



Beth Potter and Georgia Taylor-Brown are still right in the mix as we enter the 10k run.

The leading group is still bunched though as they start the first of four laps on the 2.5km running course.

On to the fourth lap of seven of the cycling disicpline, and there is a leading group of 10.

Team GB’s Beth Potter and Georgia Taylor-Brown are two athletes to be rotating the lead at the moment, but many are struggling in the slippery conditions.

Simone Biles revealed o to reporters the USA gymnastics team's nickname after gold in the group competition on Tuesday.

"F.A.A.F.O.," Biles said before one of her teammates confirmed it meant "F*** around and find out," prompting laughter in the press room.

Biles later took to social media to clarify they did have al alternative name: "Okay on the real though, the official team name is golden girls," she said.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Derek Chisorahas hit out at Algerian boxer Imane Khelif being allowed to compete in the women's category at theOlympics.

Khelif, and and Lin Yu-Ting of Taiwan, are cleared to compete by the International Olympic Committee despite being deemed biologically male after a DNA test last year.The 40-year-old, who defeated Joe Joyce on points last weekend, shared a video on X of Khelif beating up an opponent alongside the caption: "Is this fair?"

Is this fair?@Olympicspic.twitter.com/MbcrNx9DUc

Welcome back to what promises to be another dramatic day at the 2024 Paris Olympics.

And after the River Seine was declared fit to race in at the 11th hour, the women's triathlon is already underway.

title: Olympics news: Event abandoned on debut as Team GB clinch two gold medals | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson, Jack McEachen
description: Express Sport brings you up to date with all the action at the 2024 Paris Olympics.
